Ferula, also known as Ferula sprucei, is a plant that has been used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ferula include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Ferula include ferulic acid, feruline, and other phenolic compounds, as well as volatile oils and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Ferula include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions.
